entries.title: How Long Should a Pomodoro Session Be? | Superfocus
entries.description: Pomodoro sessions are typically 25 minutes. Learn why 25 min works, when to use 15 or 45 min, and try Superfocus free.
entries.h1: How long should a Pomodoro session be?
entries.answer: The classic Pomodoro session is 25 minutes of focused work, followed by a 5-minute break. After 4 sessions, take a 15-minute longer break. This comes from the Pomodoro Technique developed by Francesco Cirillo. Superfocus supports 25 min (Pomodoro), 15 min (Sprint), 45 min (Flow), and 90 min (Deep Work)—so you can match your attention span. Try it free.
entries.preset: Pomodoro (25 min) or Sprint (15 min) or Flow (45 min)

faq.q: How long should a Pomodoro session be?
faq.a: The classic is 25 minutes. Superfocus offers 15 min (Sprint), 25 min (Pomodoro), 45 min (Flow), and 90 min (Deep Work) to match your focus style.
25 min works for many people—it's long enough for deep work but short enough to avoid burnout. If 25 min feels too long, try Sprint (15 min); if too short, try Flow (45 min).

entries.title: Best Pomodoro Length - 15, 25, or 45 Minutes? | Superfocus
entries.description: Best Pomodoro length: 25 min for most, 15 min for ADHD/quick tasks, 45 min for deep work. Try Superfocus presets free.
entries.h1: What is the best Pomodoro length?
entries.answer: The best Pomodoro length depends on you. 25 minutes works for most people—it aligns with attention cycles and avoids burnout. Use 15 minutes (Sprint) for ADHD, quick tasks, or when 25 min feels too long. Use 45 minutes (Flow) or 90 minutes (Deep Work) for extended focus. Superfocus has all presets built in. Try them free.

faq.q: What is the best Pomodoro length?
faq.a: 25 min works for most. Try 15 min (Sprint) if you need shorter blocks, or 45 min (Flow) for deep work.

entries.title: Pomodoro vs Flowtime - Which Focus Method? | Superfocus
entries.description: Pomodoro vs Flowtime: fixed 25 min vs flexible 45+ min. Compare methods and try both in Superfocus free.
entries.h1: Pomodoro vs Flowtime: which is better?
entries.answer: Pomodoro uses fixed 25-minute work blocks with 5-minute breaks. Flowtime uses longer, flexible blocks (45+ min) with breaks when you need them. Pomodoro gives structure; Flowtime supports flow state. Superfocus has both: Pomodoro (25/5/15) and Flow (45/8/25). Try each and see what fits. Try Superfocus free.

faq.q: Pomodoro vs Flowtime: which is better?
faq.a: Pomodoro for structure; Flowtime for longer deep work. Superfocus has both presets. Try each.
Flowtime uses longer work blocks (45+ min) with flexible breaks. Fewer interruptions, more flow.
Yes. Pomodoro (25/5/15) and Flow (45/8/25) are built in. Free to try.

entries.title: How Many Pomodoros Per Day? Optimal Count | Superfocus
entries.description: How many Pomodoros per day? 8-12 is common. Depends on block length and energy. Track with Superfocus.
entries.h1: How many Pomodoros per day should I do?
entries.answer: There's no magic number. 8–12 Pomodoros (25 min each) is common for a full workday—that's 4–6 hours of focus. With 15-min Sprints, you might do 16–20. With 45-min Flow blocks, 4–6. It depends on your energy and tasks. Superfocus tracks your sessions so you can find your sweet spot. Try it free.

faq.a: 8–12 (25 min each) is common. Adjust based on your block length and energy. Track with Superfocus.

entries.title: Pomodoro Break Length - Short & Long Breaks | Superfocus
entries.description: Pomodoro break length: 5 min short, 15 min long (after 4 sessions). Why breaks matter. Try Superfocus free.
entries.h1: How long should Pomodoro breaks be?
entries.answer: In the classic Pomodoro: 5 minutes after each 25-min session, 15–30 minutes after 4 sessions. Short breaks let you recover without losing context; long breaks prevent burnout. Superfocus uses 5/15 by default and supports custom break lengths. Try it free.

faq.q: How long should Pomodoro breaks be?
faq.a: 5 min after each session; 15–30 min after 4 sessions. Superfocus has this built in.
Not recommended. Breaks help sustain focus and prevent burnout. Take them.

entries.title: How to Enter Flow State - Focus Timer Tips | Superfocus
entries.description: How to enter flow state: longer blocks, fewer interruptions, ambient sound. Use Flow or Deep Work preset. Try Superfocus.
entries.h1: How do I enter flow state?
entries.answer: Flow state needs longer blocks and fewer interruptions. Use Superfocus Flow (45 min) or Deep Work (90 min) instead of Pomodoro. Add lofi or rain to block noise. Clear distractions, pick one task, start the timer. The structure + sound helps your brain shift into flow. Try it free.

faq.q: How do I enter flow state?
faq.a: Use longer blocks (45 or 90 min), ambient sound, and fewer interruptions. Superfocus Flow and Deep Work presets help.
Flow (45 min) or Deep Work (90 min). Fewer breaks, more sustained focus.
